metadata.permit_type: Land Use ReviewType 1 procedure newHR - Historic Resource Review
metadata.work_description: Approval of exterior alterations at the Historic Landmark Pittock Block building, including five stainless steel eyebolt attachments through flat terra cotta tiles located in a band between the mezzanine and second story windows on the building¿s east façade to support new catenary light fixtures that will hang over SW 9th Ave between Darcelle XV Plaza and the Pittock Block, catenary light fixtures will match those used at the Ritz Carlton tower one block to the south, and decorative diamond-shaped, polished-aluminum tiles that will be suspended from the catenary wires, per the approved site plans, Exhibits C.1 through C.6, signed and dated 11/05/2025, subject to the following conditions: A.A finalized [zoning or building] permit must be obtained to document the approved project. As part of the application submittal, the following development-related conditions (B through D) must be noted on each of the site plans or included as a separate sheet. The sheet on which this information appears must be labeled "ZONING COMPLIANCE PAGE - Case File LU 25-074478 HR." All requirements must be graphically represented on the site plan, landscape, or other required plan and must be labeled "REQUIRED." B.At the time of permit submittal, a signed Certificate of Compliance form (https://www.portland.gov/ppd/documents/design-and-historic-resource-review-approvals-certificate-compliance) must be submitted to ensure the permit plans comply with the Design/Historic Resource Review decision and approved exhibits. C.No field changes allowed. D.The anchors shall penetrate the glazed architectural terra cotta tiles either only through the tile/mortar joints or through the center of each impacted tile. When formulating the means and methods, the applicant will strongly consider using an impact driver rather than a hammer drill.
